description_long: This object consists of two large, rectangular barn structures positioned side by side. Both barns feature steeply pitched gable roofs, with the left barn having a darker, more textured roof and prominent overhanging beams extending from the eaves. The right barn is larger, with a simpler, muted roof and a more expansive footprint. The exterior siding of both barns is vertically oriented, resembling traditional board and batten cladding. Large, open entrances are visible at the gable ends, suggesting easy access for vehicles or livestock. The overall appearance is rustic and utilitarian, typical of agricultural buildings designed for storage or shelter.
description: A pair of rustic, gable-roofed barns with vertical siding and large open entrances, featuring traditional agricultural design elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing both barns side by side, highlighting the open gable entrances and the overhanging beams on the left barn.
description_view_1: Rear view emphasizing the length and roof surfaces of both barns, with minimal visible openings.
description_view_2: Opposite gable end view, showing the open entrances and the depth of each barn, with the left barn's beams clearly visible.
description_view_3: Back view focusing on the rear walls and the extended beams of the left barn, with both roofs dominating the view.
attribute_door_style: large double open entrances at gable ends
attribute_window_arrangement: no visible windows on exterior walls
